1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"sort"
12		"strings"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
16		ddxconfig "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
17		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
18		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
19		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
20		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
21	)
22	
23	// ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind is the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
24	// kind appended to the bead when the post-merge reviewer was routed to the
25	// same provider as the implementer. R4 (different reviewer) is best-effort
26	// emergent: bumping MinPower usually picks a different candidate, but the
27	// guarantee is not absolute. The event surfaces the degradation so operators
28	// can see when reviewer diversity collapsed.
29	const ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind = "review-pairing-degraded"
30	
31	// ImplementerRouting captures the implementer's resolved routing details so
32	// the post-merge reviewer can build a paired ExecuteRequest with Role=reviewer,
33	// the same correlation, and MinPower bumped one above the implementer's
34	// actual selected power (R4 pairing).
35	type ImplementerRouting struct {
36		Harness     string
37		Provider    string
38		Model       string
39		ActualPower int
40		// Correlation is the implementer's correlation map. The reviewer copies
41		// its keys into its own dispatch metadata so events / session log /
42		// aggregations can join the two calls.
43		Correlation map[string]string
44	}
45	
46	// Verdict is the outcome of a post-merge bead review.
47	type Verdict string
48	
49	const (
50		// VerdictApprove means all AC items passed; the bead stays closed.
51		VerdictApprove Verdict = "APPROVE"
52		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead is reopened.
53		VerdictRequestChanges Verdict = "REQUEST_CHANGES"
54		// VerdictBlock means escalation should stop; the bead is flagged for human review.
55		VerdictBlock Verdict = "BLOCK"
56	)
57	
58	// ReviewResult is the structured outcome of a post-merge bead review.
59	type ReviewResult struct {
60		Verdict          Verdict    `json:"verdict"`
61		Rationale        string     `json:"rationale,omitempty"`
62		PerAC            []ReviewAC `json:"per_ac,omitempty"`
63		RawOutput        string     `json:"raw_output,omitempty"`
64		ReviewerHarness  string     `json:"reviewer_harness,omitempty"`
65		ReviewerModel    string     `json:"reviewer_model,omitempty"`
66		ReviewerProvider string     `json:"reviewer_provider,omitempty"`
67		SessionID        string     `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
68		BaseRev          string     `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
69		ResultRev        string     `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
70		ExecutionDir     string     `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
71		DurationMS       int        `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
72		Error            string     `json:"error,omitempty"`
73		// InputBytes and OutputBytes are the FEAT-022 §16 byte counters used to
74		// populate the compact summary appended to review and review-error event
75		// bodies. InputBytes is the assembled prompt size; OutputBytes is the
76		// raw reviewer output size (zero on pre-dispatch overflow / transport).
77		InputBytes  int `json:"input_bytes,omitempty"`
78		OutputBytes int `json:"output_bytes,omitempty"`
79	}
80	
81	type ReviewAC struct {
82		Number   int    `json:"number"`
83		Item     string `json:"item,omitempty"`
84		Grade    string `json:"grade,omitempty"`
85		Evidence string `json:"evidence,omitempty"`
86	}
87	
88	type reviewArtifactManifest struct {
89		Harness          string                     `json:"harness,omitempty"`
90		Model            string                     `json:"model,omitempty"`
91		SessionID        string                     `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
92		BaseRev          string                     `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
93		ResultRev        string                     `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
94		Verdict          string                     `json:"verdict,omitempty"`
95		BeadID           string                     `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
96		ExecutionDir     string                     `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
97		EvidenceAssembly *EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry `json:"evidence_assembly,omitempty"`
98	}
99	
100	type reviewArtifactResult struct {
101		Verdict          string                     `json:"verdict"`
102		PerAC            []ReviewAC                 `json:"per_ac,omitempty"`
103		Rationale        string                     `json:"rationale,omitempty"`
104		Findings         []Finding                  `json:"findings,omitempty"`
105		Error            string                     `json:"error,omitempty"`
106		EvidenceAssembly *EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry `json:"evidence_assembly,omitempty"`
107	}
108	
109	// SelectReviewerTier returns the tier to use for the review agent.
110	// Rule: max(impl_tier + 1, smart). Since smart is the ceiling, the
111	// reviewer always runs at smart tier regardless of the implementation tier.
112	func SelectReviewerTier(_ escalation.ModelTier) escalation.ModelTier {
113		return escalation.TierSmart
114	}
115	
116	// HasBeadLabel reports whether label is present in labels.
117	func HasBeadLabel(labels []string, label string) bool {
118		for _, l := range labels {
119			if l == label {
120				return true
121			}
122		}
123		return false
124	}
125	
126	// BeadReader can fetch a bead by ID. Implemented by *bead.Store.
127	type BeadReader interface {
128		Get(id string) (*bead.Bead, error)
129	}
130	
131	// BeadReviewer runs a post-merge review for a completed bead.
132	type BeadReviewer interface {
133		ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error)
134	}
135	
136	// BeadReviewerFunc is a functional adapter implementing BeadReviewer.
137	type BeadReviewerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error)
138	
139	func (f BeadReviewerFunc) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
140		return f(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
141	}
142	
143	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
144	// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
145	// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
146	// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
147	// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
148	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
149	
150	For each acceptance-criteria (AC) item, decide whether it is implemented correctly, then assign one overall verdict:
151	
152	- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
153	- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
154	- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented; or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
155	
156	## Required output format (schema_version: 1)
157	
158	Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:
159	
160	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
161	{
162	  "schema_version": 1,
163	  "verdict": "APPROVE",
164	  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
165	  "findings": [
166	    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
167	  ]
168	}
169	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `
170	
171	Rules:
172	- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK".
173	- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
174	- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
175	- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the words APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`
176	
177	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
178	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
179	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
180	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
181		Caps evidence.Caps
182	}
183	
184	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
185	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
186	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
187	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
188	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
189		Prompt   string
190		Overflow bool
191		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
192	}
193	
194	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
195	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
196	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
197	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
198		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
199	}
200	
201	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
202	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
203	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
204	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
205	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
206	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
207	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
208	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
209		caps := opts.Caps
210		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
211			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
212		}
213	
214		var sb strings.Builder
215		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
216	
217		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
218	
219		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
220		beadStart := sb.Len()
221		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
222		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
223	
224		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
225			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
226		} else {
227			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
228		}
229	
230		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
231			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
232		} else {
233			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
234		}
235	
236		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
237			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
238		}
239	
240		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
241			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
242		}
243	
244		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
245		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
246			Name:          "bead",
247			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
248			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
249		})
250	
251		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
252		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
253		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
254			invStart := sb.Len()
255			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
256			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
257			for _, f := range allFiles {
258				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
259				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
260			}
261			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
262			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
263				Name:          "changed-files",
264				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
265				SelectedItems: paths,
266			})
267		}
268	
269		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
270		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
271		if len(refs) == 0 {
272			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
273		} else {
274			for _, ref := range refs {
275				if ref.Title != "" {
276					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
277				} else {
278					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
279				}
280				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
281				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
282				if readErr != nil {
283					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
284					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
285						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
286						TruncationReason: "read_error",
287						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
288					})
289				} else {
290					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
291					if truncated {
292						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
293					}
294					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", txt)
295					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
296						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
297						BytesIncluded: len(content),
298						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
299					}
300					if truncated {
301						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
302						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
303					}
304					sections = append(sections, sec)
305				}
306				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
307			}
308		}
309		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
310	
311		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
312		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
313		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
314		diffSection.Name = "diff"
315		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
316		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n"))
317	
318		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
319		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
320		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
321		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
322	
323		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
324	
325		out := sb.String()
326		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
327			Prompt:   out,
328			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
329			Sections: sections,
330		}
331	}
332	
333	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
334	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
335	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
336	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
337	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
338	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
339	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
340		if len(files) == 0 {
341			return originalDiff
342		}
343		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
344			if f.Path == "" {
345				return 3
346			}
347			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
348				return 0
349			}
350			for _, r := range refs {
351				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
352					return 1
353				}
354			}
355			return 2
356		}
357		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
358		copy(ordered, files)
359		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
360	
361		var b2 strings.Builder
362		for _, f := range ordered {
363			body := f.Body
364			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
365				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
366			}
367			b2.WriteString(body)
368		}
369		return b2.String()
370	}
371	
372	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
373	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
374	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
375	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
376	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
377		var b strings.Builder
378		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
379		b.WriteString(f.Path)
380		b.WriteString(" b/")
381		b.WriteString(f.Path)
382		b.WriteString("\n")
383		if f.Stat != "" {
384			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
385			b.WriteString("\n")
386		}
387		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
388			b.WriteString(h)
389			b.WriteString("\n")
390		}
391		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
392		return b.String()
393	}
394	
395	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
396	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
397	// exact symbolic resolution).
398	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
399		if text == "" || p == "" {
400			return false
401		}
402		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
403			return true
404		}
405		base := path.Base(p)
406		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
407	}
408	
409	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
410	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
411	// documents.
412	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
413		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
414			return false
415		}
416		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
417		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
418			return true
419		}
420		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
421			return true
422		}
423		return false
424	}
425	
426	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
427	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
428		var buf bytes.Buffer
429		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
430			"&", "&",
431			"<", "<",
432			">", ">",
433		).Replace(s))
434		return buf.String()
435	}
436	
437	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
438	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
439	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
440	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
441		ProjectRoot string
442		BeadStore   BeadReader
443		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
444		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
445		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
446		Service agentlib.FizeauService
447		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
448		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
449		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
450		Runner AgentRunner
451		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
452		// When empty, Harness defaults to "claude" and Model is resolved
453		// from TierSmart for the chosen harness.
454		Harness string
455		Model   string
456		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
457		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
458		// applies.
459		Caps evidence.Caps
460		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
461		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved
462		// provider matches the implementer's. Optional: nil disables the
463		// telemetry emission while preserving the rest of the review flow.
464		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
465	}
466	
467	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
468	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It pins the reviewer harness/model, attaches
469	// the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid), and
470	// derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
471	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
472	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
473	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
474	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerModel string) AgentRunRuntime {
475		correlation := map[string]string{}
476		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
477			correlation[k] = v
478		}
479		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
480		if impl.Harness != "" {
481			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
482		}
483		if impl.Provider != "" {
484			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
485		}
486		if impl.Model != "" {
487			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
488		}
489		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
490			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
491		}
492		minPower := 0
493		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
494			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
495		}
496		return AgentRunRuntime{
497			HarnessOverride:  reviewerHarness,
498			ModelOverride:    reviewerModel,
499			MinPowerOverride: minPower,
500			Correlation:      correlation,
501		}
502	}
503	
504	// reviewPairingDegradedBody renders the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
505	// body. The body carries both implementer and reviewer routing details so
506	// operators can see exactly which routes converged.
507	func reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProvider, reviewerModel string, reviewerPower int) string {
508		var b strings.Builder
509		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_harness=%s\n", impl.Harness)
510		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_provider=%s\n", impl.Provider)
511		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_model=%s\n", impl.Model)
512		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_actual_power=%d\n", impl.ActualPower)
513		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_harness=%s\n", reviewerHarness)
514		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_provider=%s\n", reviewerProvider)
515		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_model=%s\n", reviewerModel)
516		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_actual_power=%d\n", reviewerPower)
517		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "min_power_requested=%d\n", impl.ActualPower+1)
518		return b.String()
519	}
520	
521	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
522	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
523		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
524		if err != nil {
525			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
526		}
527	
528		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
529		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
530		if err != nil {
531			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
532		}
533	
534		// Resolve governing document references.
535		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
536	
537		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
538		iter := 1
539	
540		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
541		caps := r.Caps
542		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
543			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
544		}
545		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
546		prompt := built.Prompt
547		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
548		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
549		if err != nil {
550			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
551		}
552		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
553			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
554		}
555	
556		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
557		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
558		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
559		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
560		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
561		if built.Overflow {
562			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
563			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
564				Sections:    built.Sections,
565				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
566				OutputBytes: 0,
567				Harness:     r.Harness,
568				Model:       r.Model,
569			}
570			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
571				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
572				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
573				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
574				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
575				ReviewerModel:   r.Model,
576				BaseRev:         baseRev,
577				ResultRev:       resultRev,
578				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
579				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
580				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
581			}
582			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
583				Harness:          r.Harness,
584				Model:            r.Model,
585				BaseRev:          baseRev,
586				ResultRev:        resultRev,
587				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
588				BeadID:           beadID,
589				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
590				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
591			}, reviewArtifactResult{
592				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
593				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
594				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
595			})
596			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
597				evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow, len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
598		}
599	
600		// Resolve reviewer harness and model. The implementer's harness is
601		// intentionally NOT used as a fallback — defaulting to it would directly
602		// violate R4 (different reviewer). When the configured reviewer harness
603		// is empty we fall through to a fixed default ("claude") regardless of
604		// what harness ran the implementer.
605		reviewHarness := r.Harness
606		if reviewHarness == "" {
607			reviewHarness = "claude"
608		}
609		reviewModel := r.Model
610		if reviewModel == "" {
611			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
612		}
613	
614		start := time.Now()
615		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewModel)
616		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
617		runRuntime.WorkDir = r.ProjectRoot
618		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
619	
620		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
621		if runErr != nil {
622			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
623			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
624			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
625			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
626				Sections:    built.Sections,
627				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
628				OutputBytes: 0,
629				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
630				Harness:     reviewHarness,
631				Model:       reviewModel,
632			}
633			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
634				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
635				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
636				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
637				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
638				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
639				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
640				ResultRev:       resultRev,
641				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
642				DurationMS:      durationMS,
643				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
644				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
645			}
646			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
647				Harness:          reviewHarness,
648				Model:            reviewModel,
649				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
650				ResultRev:        resultRev,
651				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
652				BeadID:           beadID,
653				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
654				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
655			}, reviewArtifactResult{
656				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
657				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
658				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
659				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
660			})
661			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
662		}
663	
664		actualHarness := reviewHarness
665		actualModel := reviewModel
666		actualProvider := ""
667		actualPower := 0
668		if result != nil {
669			if result.Harness != "" {
670				actualHarness = result.Harness
671			}
672			if result.Model != "" {
673				actualModel = result.Model
674			}
675			actualProvider = result.Provider
676			actualPower = result.ActualPower
677			durationMS = result.DurationMS
678		}
679	
680		output := ""
681		sessionID := ""
682		if result != nil {
683			output = result.Output
684			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
685		}
686		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
687		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
688		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
689		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop reopens the bead for
690		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
691		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
692		var strictVerdict Verdict
693		var rationale string
694		var findings []Finding
695		if parseErr == nil {
696			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
697			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
698			findings = parsed.Findings
699			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
700				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
701				for _, f := range findings {
702					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
703					if line == "" {
704						continue
705					}
706					if f.Location != "" {
707						line = f.Location + ": " + line
708					}
709					parts = append(parts, line)
710				}
711				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
712			}
713		}
714		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
715		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
716			Sections:    built.Sections,
717			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
718			OutputBytes: len(output),
719			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
720			Harness:     actualHarness,
721			Model:       actualModel,
722		}
723		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
724			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
725			Rationale:        rationale,
726			RawOutput:        output,
727			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
728			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
729			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
730			SessionID:        sessionID,
731			BaseRev:          baseRev,
732			ResultRev:        resultRev,
733			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
734			DurationMS:       durationMS,
735			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
736			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
737		}
738	
739		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
740		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
741		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
742		// regression. Best-effort only — emission failures never affect the
743		// review outcome itself.
744		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
745			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
746				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
747				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
748				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower),
749				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
750				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
751			})
752		}
753		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
754			Harness:          actualHarness,
755			Model:            actualModel,
756			SessionID:        sessionID,
757			BaseRev:          baseRev,
758			ResultRev:        resultRev,
759			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
760			BeadID:           beadID,
761			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
762			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
763		}, reviewArtifactResult{
764			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
765			Rationale:        rationale,
766			Findings:         findings,
767			Error:            reviewRes.Error,
768			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
769		})
770		if parseErr != nil {
771			// FEAT-022 §12: distinguish provider_empty (zero bytes) from
772			// unparseable (text without a recognizable JSON contract). Operators
773			// triage these differently — provider_empty often signals a context-
774			// window or backend availability issue, while unparseable signals a
775			// reviewer-prompt or output-format drift.
776			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
777			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
778				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
779			}
780			reviewRes.Error = class
781			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
782		}
783		return reviewRes, nil
784	}
785	
786	// dispatchReviewRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
787	// for the post-merge reviewer. The reviewer carries no persistent
788	// ResolvedConfig of its own — the durable knobs that affect a review
789	// invocation (timeout, provider, evidence caps) are read from the project's
790	// .ddx/config.yaml via LoadAndResolve, while reviewer-tier harness/model
791	// are pinned via the runtime override fields. Resolution order matches the
792	// execute-bead worker (runner > pre-built service > fresh service).
793	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
794		rcfg, _ := ddxconfig.LoadAndResolve(r.ProjectRoot, ddxconfig.CLIOverrides{})
795		return dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, r.Service, r.Runner, rcfg, runtime)
796	}
797	
798	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
799	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
800	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
801	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
802	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
803		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
804		defer cancel()
805		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
806		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
807		if err != nil {
808			return "", err
809		}
810		return string(out), nil
811	}
812	
813	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
814		if resultRev == "" {
815			return ""
816		}
817		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
818		if err != nil {
819			return ""
820		}
821		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
822	}
823	
824	func writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, manifest reviewArtifactManifest, result reviewArtifactResult) error {
825		if artifacts == nil {
826			return nil
827		}
828		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ManifestAbs, manifest); err != nil {
829			return err
830		}
831		return writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, result)
832	}
833	
834	func ReadReviewArtifactResult(path string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
835		//evidence:allow-unbounded — review result artifacts are small JSON
836		// documents written by writeReviewArtifacts; bounded by construction.
837		raw, err := os.ReadFile(path)
838		if err != nil {
839			return nil, err
840		}
841		var artifact reviewArtifactResult
842		if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &artifact); err != nil {
843			return nil, err
844		}
845		return &ReviewResult{
846			Verdict:   Verdict(artifact.Verdict),
847			Rationale: artifact.Rationale,
848			PerAC:     artifact.PerAC,
849			Error:     artifact.Error,
850		}, nil
851	}
852

data.output:
commit 0dd8a81710495742decf1ed52dfd77e522011dac
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 09:14:55 2026 -0400

    fix(review): R4 pairing — drop impl-harness fallback, emit pairing-degraded [ddx-2436fa73]
    
    Reviewer no longer defaults to the implementer harness when its own Harness
    is unset (that path violated R4: different reviewer). The reviewer
    ExecuteRequest now carries Role=reviewer + the implementer's correlation map +
    MinPower=actualPower+1 so Fizeau biases routing toward a stronger candidate.
    When the reviewer still resolves to the same provider as the implementer, a
    typed kind:review-pairing-degraded event is appended so operators can see the
    diversity collapse.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

data.text: The implementation is already in HEAD (commit 0dd8a817). All four ACs are satisfied with passing tests. Writing rationale for the no-changes outcome.

input.content:
Bead ddx-2436fa73 — no code changes required this attempt.

Status: All acceptance criteria are already satisfied by commit 0dd8a817
("fix(review): R4 pairing — drop impl-harness fallback, emit pairing-degraded
[ddx-2436fa73]"), which is an ancestor of this worktree's base
(10331745 chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260502T135553-29fca310).

The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note on this bead was a false positive: the reviewer
saw a merge whose diff contained only a result.json artifact, but the actual
source changes had already landed in commit 0dd8a817 against
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go and 17 sibling files
(including a 213-line execute_bead_review_pairing_test.go).

AC ↔ evidence mapping (all in cli/internal/agent/):

AC1 — execute_bead_review.go no longer defaults to implementer harness.
  Evidence: execute_bead_review.go:600-608 — when r.Harness is empty the
  reviewer falls through to the literal "claude", explicitly NOT to
  impl.Harness; comment at 601-604 documents the R4 rationale.
  Test: TestReviewBead_NoLongerDefaultsToImplementerHarness — PASS.

AC2 — Reviewer call carries Role='reviewer' + same CorrelationID +
       MinPower = impl.ActualPower + 1.
  Evidence: BuildReviewExecuteRequest at execute_bead_review.go:474-502
  copies impl.Correlation, overlays role=reviewer, and sets
  MinPowerOverride = impl.ActualPower + 1.
  Tests: TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest,
         TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest_ZeroPowerLeavesMinPowerZero — PASS.

AC3 — review-pairing-degraded event emitted when reviewer's
       RoutingActual.Provider == implementer's.
  Evidence: ReviewBead at execute_bead_review.go:744-752 appends a
  bead.BeadEvent with Kind=ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind
  ("review-pairing-degraded", declared at line 29) when
  actualProvider == impl.Provider.
  Test: TestReviewBead_DegradedPath_SameProvider_EmitsEvent — PASS.

AC4 — Tests cover happy / degraded / event payload with both routing
       details.
  Evidence: execute_bead_review_pairing_test.go provides
  TestReviewBead_HappyPath_DifferentProvider_NoDegradedEvent (no event
  emitted), TestReviewBead_DegradedPath_SameProvider_EmitsEvent (event
  emitted, body asserted to contain impl_* and reviewer_* fields via
  reviewPairingDegradedBody at execute_bead_review.go:507-519), and
  TestReviewBead_NoLongerDefaultsToImplementerHarness. All PASS.

Verification run (this worktree):
  go test ./internal/agent/ -run "ReviewBead|BuildReview|PairingDegraded|ReviewPairing" -count=1
  -> 13/13 PASS, ok.

Conclusion: nothing to implement. The reopen was driven by a stale review
of a merge that landed evidence-only after the actual fix; the source-of-
truth code in HEAD already meets the contract. No commit produced this
attempt; this rationale is the evidence artifact.

data.text: All four ACs are already satisfied by commit `0dd8a817` (ancestor of this worktree's base). Verified by running the relevant tests in `cli/internal/agent/` — 13/13 PASS including `TestReviewBead_NoLongerDefaultsToImplementerHarness`, `TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest` (Role=reviewer + correlation + MinPower=actualPower+1), and `TestReviewBead_DegradedPath_SameProvider_EmitsEvent`. The prior REVIEW:BLOCK was a false positive against a merge that contained only an evidence file. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` mapping each AC to its evidence in `execute_bead_review.go` and the corresponding passing test.
